what: The authored spec-validator selection scenario asserting that a trade the judge can rule against is ruled on rather than escalated shared a byte-identical When with a frozen sibling four lines up and asserted the byte-level negation of its Then. It was aimed at that sibling's apparatus by construction: its Given required a recorded trade the producer accepts, and the corpus's only producer-accepted rubric is the schema-migration exemplar a separate frozen scenario mandates. The tri-state dodge that keeps the escalation sibling disjoint does not extend to a Then that exhausts the same predicate. Re-authored both new judge scenarios onto their own apparatus in domains the corpus does not illustrate, and tightened the Given from can-say-it-does-not-accept to can-rule-that-it-rejects, which the loose negation had conflated with the undecided case.

The bar asserted the trade-recording duty and the suite froze only its happy path and its exemption, leaving the violation case and the revise arm of author-or-revise unspecified. Added two producer scenarios binding the duty on a fresh authoring and on a rewritten standing dimension, both phrased on the suite's before-returning idiom rather than a completeness gate, which would have contradicted the frozen scenario whose Given enumerates the other return-gate duties and does not enumerate this one.

The bar stated that the record buys reviewability and not detection in both the selection section and the soft-spot section that section forward-references. Collapsed the first to the pointer, leaving the claim stated once.

The two authored scenarios binding the recording duty named a dimension carrying no recorded trade without requiring that its trade be one the producer accepts. A non-substitutable criterion satisfied that Given, and the Then would then have forced a trade record onto a criterion the selection rule requires to leave the sum entirely — asserting the opposite of the rule the scenarios exist to freeze. Narrowed both Givens to a dimension whose trade the producer accepts, leaving the duty bound only to dimensions that legitimately belong in the sum.

The judge-side instruction said a dimension the CR authors or revises records its trade and that a STANDING dimension recording none is not reportable. The standing qualifier was load-bearing: it implied an authored dimension recording no trade IS reportable, which flips the verdict of a frozen judge scenario that passes a rubric whose dimensions a reviewer would genuinely trade. That scenario is silent on records, so on the base every snapshot it covered records none and the rule narrowed its entire original extension with its text untouched — a semantic narrowing firing Clearance, structurally invisible to addOnly. The prior round had moved this collision out of a scenario and into governance prose rather than removing it. Foreclosed the reading on the merits: a record's presence is a lexical fact, not a judgment about the domain, so no judge reports a missing record whoever authored it; the duty is the producer's alone and its mechanical check is deferred behind the corpus backfill. The frozen scenario keeps its full extension and the CR stays genuinely additive.

The judge-side instruction to grade a recorded trade as a stated proposition flipped a frozen judge scenario that passes a rubric whose dimensions a reviewer would genuinely trade. That scenario's Then is a NEGATIVE — it does not report failing — so ANY new selection-failure ground reaches it, and scoping the rule to records that state the trade for the dimension under selection does not escape: a well-formed trade over that scenario's own dimensions which a judge rejects is constructible. No scoped judge-side grading rule survives; the third round of foreclosing would have migrated the seam a fourth time. Removed the record from the judge's path entirely, which the very first adversarial read had argued independently on anchoring grounds: a producer's account of its own trade is not evidence, and grading it converts an independent re-derivation into a review of the producer's argument. The judge now re-derives selection from the dimensions exactly as it did on the base, gaining no failure ground; the record's audience is the owner, matching the cut's recorded reason, which no judge grades either. Re-authored the judge scenarios onto the judge's own substitutability ruling and froze the anti-narrowing case: a rubric carrying a record still passes, and the record is never itself reported.

The authored bar claimed that correcting the standing corpus carries the threshold re-derivation any such correction forces, conflating two different corrections. Recording a trade adds prose beside the rubric and changes no dimension maximum and no cut, so it forces no re-derivation; stripping a non-substitutable dimension is what does. Corrected to say so.

The authored duty said to record the trade beside the rubric, while the threshold section four paragraphs down permits the cut's reason to live either beside the rubric or in the node's spec body. Since the CR's own rule puts both in one record, the two instructions pointed at different places and the new sentence silently withdrew a standing permission. An authored scenario hard-coded the location into its assertion as well. Deferred the location to the existing permission at every site and dropped it from the scenario's assertion.

The CR asserts across four mirrored files that a producer's account of its own trade is not evidence and never fails or rescues a dimension, but the only scenario exercising a present record had the judge's independent derivation agreeing with it. The adversarial direction — a dimension the judge rejects on its own, carrying a record arguing for acceptance — was untested, which is precisely the anchoring case the issue worries about and the CR's own thesis. Added it on fresh apparatus. It adds no failure ground, since a non-substitutable dimension already fails, and asserts only that the record does not settle the question.
